Alright, welcome back to lesson two of module two. This one is all about the three dimensional ever evolving avatar. In the last lesson, you know, we learned that our prospect must be engaged on this transformative journey between point A and point B. And we also saw that there are seven major milestones on that path as well as three state checks to move them along in a smooth reaffirming way. And that this journey all starts with validation, this recognition of where they're currently at and culminates with this powerful sense of vindication, this sense of being exonerated from all past actions and fears and inaction to the relates to the problem that they're trying to solve. So these are all some very strong human emotions, which of course, if we're going to do them successfully require, well, a living breathing human, you know, we've got to make it alive. And you know, I know that a lot of you have already been doing some work with empathy mapping and customer personas, and these things aren't necessarily unfamiliar to you. And I want to just mention that what we're doing here isn't to detract from any of that at all. What we're doing isn't contradictory, but actually complimentary. And the same goes for the whole quantitative versus qualitative debate. You know, there are some amazing lessons and 10x emails and some other blog posts throughout copy hackers that teach you how to gather data through surveys and other types of data mining methods. And that's all amazing. We're not discounting from that at all. What we're doing here is we're simply breathing extra life. We're drilling deeper into it, going below the surface and generating the ahas and insights that won't show up necessarily on a spreadsheet, a customer response or a standard empathy map. So what we're doing is it's about taking these personas, these data points, these avatars that you've created and actually making them real for you. Empathy mapping is a great start, but how many of us have been guilty of doing the exercise once, creating this two dimensional avatar with a stock image of some guy named Tony, and then having it hibernate in a Google Doc forever. You know, cold, weathered, starving to death in some kind of pixelized hell. So that's what we're moving away from. Obviously, two dimensional Tony isn't very useful to us when we're trying to create messaging for our funnel that connects on those deepest levels and inspires a very real, and I keep using and empathize, emphasizing the word real transformation for very real people. This is something that I tend to have hanging on my office bulletin board every time I start slipping into that sense of my customer not being real. If it bleeds, we can sell it. That is predator. I think it was Arnold or Carl Weathers or one of them that dropped a line in that movie when they first discovered that this thing, this creature could bleed. If it bleeds, we can kill it, I think they said, but if it bleeds, we can sell it. So it's our job here to make our avatar three dimensional to fill it in with flesh, blood, bone, guts, and a heartbeat that fuels the journey. And this is one thing I said in a workshop in Sonoma to one of our mastermind groups, and it was totally spontaneous at the moment when I was just on the fly unpacking my own writing process and what I go through on a very visceral level when writing for not even my own audience, but my clients audiences. I simply said I don't write until I feel like, expensive. You know, and until I've so deeply embodied and identified with the person I'm talking to and until I truly feel empathy, not just as a loaded buzzword, but my own lived reality when I'm so desperate to solve their problems because their problems and pain have become my own, you know, and that's where the best messaging comes from. And it may sound esoteric or inaccessible, but the exercise I'm going to leave you in later in this lesson will actually start to ground this and something you can use right away. And I suggest you do these things before starting any campaign, especially if you're a copywriter working for clients and have to identify with an audience as quickly and effectively as possible. Because at the end of the day, empathy is one of the greatest gifts you can give another human, but it doesn't belong on a map. It's real. It's accessible. And to the greater degree that you choose, that you voluntarily choose to tap into it and then express it on the page, the greater you'll connect, the greater your ability to convert and the greater you will be as a conversion coach. So deep breaths. I have two exercises waiting for you in the next lesson. Get something warm. Get a good coffee. Get the blood flowing and I will meet you there in the next lesson in just a minute.
